title: Venture Radar VC Recommendation Packet
platform_pitch: I built Venture Radar, a source-backed venture intelligence system that ingests public startup signals, deduplicates companies, scores them with explainable criteria, and maps each company against 12 VC thesis profiles. The current run screened 13 tracked companies and generated firm-specific recommendations with evidence, pass risks, and diligence questions.

firm.name: 8VC
firm.thesis: Build and invest in ambitious technology platforms that create lasting economic and societal value.

recommended_companies.name: Expanse
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_care: Expanse is an AI infrastructure company around HPC/GPU cluster utilization and scheduler-aware resource prediction. That maps to 8VC's AI infrastructure focus. Launch HN engagement: 103 points and 28 comments when fetched. Source text includes paid-pilot or customer-pilot language. Source text includes a usage-scale claim that needs verification. Treat HN engagement as community interest, not revenue or customer traction.
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_pass: 8VC may pass if diligence cannot verify the company was extracted from one launch source; core claims need corroboration.
recommended_companies.suggested_angle: Lead with Expanse's HPC/GPU cluster utilization and scheduler-aware resource prediction, then show the source evidence and the first diligence question: Which clusters are installed or piloting today, and what utilization recovery has been measured?
recommended_companies.diligence_question: Which clusters are installed or piloting today, and what utilization recovery has been measured?

recommended_companies.name: Transload
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_care: Transload is a Logistics and supply chain company around computer-vision freight dimensioning in logistics terminals. That maps to 8VC's Logistics focus.
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_pass: 8VC may pass if diligence cannot verify the company was extracted from one launch source; core claims need corroboration.
recommended_companies.suggested_angle: Frame Transload as a possible thesis-adjacent lead around computer-vision freight dimensioning in logistics terminals; be explicit about the open diligence question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?
recommended_companies.diligence_question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?

recommended_companies.name: Minicor
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_care: Minicor is an AI agents and workflow automation company around desktop RPA orchestration for systems without APIs. That maps to 8VC's Enterprise SaaS focus. Launch HN engagement: 105 points and 64 comments when fetched. Source text includes paid-pilot or customer-pilot language. Treat HN engagement as community interest, not revenue or customer traction.
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_pass: 8VC may pass if diligence cannot verify the company was extracted from one launch source; core claims need corroboration.
recommended_companies.suggested_angle: Frame Minicor as a possible thesis-adjacent lead around desktop RPA orchestration for systems without APIs; be explicit about the open diligence question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?
recommended_companies.diligence_question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?

recommended_companies.name: Chert
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_care: Chert is a Communications infrastructure company around programmatic iMessage and messaging infrastructure for business workflows. That maps to 8VC's Enterprise SaaS focus. Launch HN engagement: 62 points and 200 comments when fetched. Source text includes paid-pilot or customer-pilot language. Source text includes a usage-scale claim that needs verification. Treat HN engagement as community interest, not revenue or customer traction.
recommended_companies.why_this_firm_might_pass: 8VC may pass if diligence cannot verify the company was extracted from one launch source; core claims need corroboration.
recommended_companies.suggested_angle: Frame Chert as a possible thesis-adjacent lead around programmatic iMessage and messaging infrastructure for business workflows; be explicit about the open diligence question: How durable is the platform dependency around iMessage delivery and line provisioning?
recommended_companies.diligence_question: How durable is the platform dependency around iMessage delivery and line provisioning?

firm_recommendations.outbound_email:
Subject: Source-backed startup recommendations for 8VC

Hi Joe Lonsdale,

I built Venture Radar, a venture intelligence system that screens public startup signals and maps companies to firm-specific thesis profiles.

For 8VC, the current run surfaced:

- Expanse (AI infrastructure) - 83/100 VC fit. Lead with Expanse's HPC/GPU cluster utilization and scheduler-aware resource prediction, then show the source evidence and the first diligence question: Which clusters are installed or piloting today, and what utilization recovery has been measured?
- Transload (Logistics and supply chain) - 67/100 VC fit. Frame Transload as a possible thesis-adjacent lead around computer-vision freight dimensioning in logistics terminals; be explicit about the open diligence question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?
- Minicor (AI agents and workflow automation) - 66/100 VC fit. Frame Minicor as a possible thesis-adjacent lead around desktop RPA orchestration for systems without APIs; be explicit about the open diligence question: What product is live today and who is using it repeatedly?

I am not treating these as investment recommendations. The output is a structured sourcing screen: evidence, fit rationale, pass risks, and diligence questions. I would value 15 minutes of feedback on whether this kind of source-backed sourcing workflow is useful for your team.

Best,
Aidan

For 8VC, it surfaced Expanse as a current fit, with evidence, pass risks, and diligence questions. I would value feedback on the system and whether this kind of sourcing workflow is useful for your team.
